name: Chewy, Inc.
business: Chewy sells pet products, supplies, prescriptions, and services online to pet parents in the United States and Canada. It partners with approximately 4,000 brands and offers about 190,000 products, including private labels. The company also provides pet health services such as telehealth, pharmacy, insurance, and veterinary clinics.
sellsTo: Pet parents in the U.S. and Canada

exposures.sentence: We believe that we are the preeminent online source for pet products, supplies and prescriptions as a result of our broad selection of high-quality products and services, which we offer at competitive prices and deliver with an exceptional level of care and a personal touch to build brand loyalty and drive repeat purchasing.
In 2020, we launched Connect with a Vet, an industry-leading telehealth service that allows pet parents to connect directly with licensed veterinarians or veterinary technicians for pet care.
In 2022, we also launched CarePlus, our product suite of Insurance and Wellness plans offering comprehensive coverage options across varying price points.
Additionally, our investments in automation and artificial intelligence (AI) technologies within our fulfillment centers drive efficiency across our distribution network.
We expect AI technologies to become increasingly important to e-commerce businesses.

catalysts.sentence: As a result, we expect that this project will go live towards the end of Fiscal Year 2026.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. pet market spending estimated at $157 billion in 2025, growing at 8.1% CAGR from 2019.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Packaged Facts, spending in the U.S. pet market has grown from $98 billion in 2019 to an estimated $157 billion in 2025, or at an 8.1% compounded annual growth rate ( CAGR ) over that time.

tamClaims.claim: Projected 4% CAGR for U.S. pet market from 2025 to 2029.
tamClaims.sentence: Packaged Facts projects the U.S. pet market to grow at an estimated CAGR of approximately 4% from 2025 through 2029.

tamClaims.claim: Online shopping share of U.S. retail pet product sales was 41% in 2025, with over $41 billion in pet food and treats sold online.
tamClaims.sentence: Packaged Facts reports that online shopping grew from 24% of U.S. retail pet product sales in 2019 to an estimated 41% in 2025 with over $41 billion of pet food and treats sold online.

tamClaims.claim: 49% of online pet product buyers used an Autoship/subscription program in the last 12 months.
tamClaims.sentence: According to a Packaged Facts survey conducted in January 2025, among those who buy pet products online, 49% have used an Autoship/subscription purchasing program within the last 12 months for pet-related products.
